div1.entryFree: Devotement, devotion, devoted love and veneration: he hath devoted and given up himself to the contemplation, mark and d. of her parts and graces, Oth. II, 3, 322 (most M. Edd., after the later Ff and Qq, denotement). The use of the prepos. of is to be accounted for by what in grammar is called Ζευ_γμα; cf. Sonn. 48, 12. Meas. II, 4, 1. John II, 27.
